title: Groundworkers
description: Perform tasks involving physical labor at construction sites. May operate hand and power tools of all types: air hammers, earth tampers, cement mixers, small mechanical hoists, surveying and measuring equipment, and a variety of other equipment and instruments. May clean and prepare sites, dig trenches, set braces to support the sides of excavations, erect scaffolding, and clean up rubble, debris, and other waste materials. May assist other craft workers. Help brickmasons, blockmasons, stonemasons, or tile and marble setters by performing duties requiring less skill. Duties include using, supplying, or holding materials or tools, and cleaning work area and equipment. Help carpenters by performing duties requiring less skill. Duties include using, supplying, or holding materials or tools, and cleaning work area and equipment. (Based on 10 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Remove damaged tile, brick, or mortar, and clean or prepare surfaces using appropriate tools.
Mix ingredients to create compounds for covering or cleaning surfaces or to mix mortar, plaster, and grout according to standard formulas.
Raze buildings or salvage useful materials, and spray materials to clean, coat, or seal surfaces as required.
Erect scaffolding or other installation structures, and position or dismantle forms for pouring concrete.
Locate and supply materials to masons for installation and transport materials, tools, or machines to installation sites.
Provide assistance in the preparation, installation, repair, or rebuilding of tile, brick, or stone surfaces, and assist other craft workers.
Clean installation surfaces, equipment, tools, work sites, or storage areas using appropriate methods and tools.
Dig ditches or trenches, backfill excavations or compact and level earth to grade specifications, using hand tools or machinery.
Measure, mark, or record openings or distances to layout areas where construction work will be performed and read plans, instructions, or specifications.
Cut materials to specified sizes for installation, drill holes, and perform semi-skilled duties related to a variety of systems.
Maintain tools and equipment, including cleaning, lubricating, or repairing machinery to ensure operability.
Perform rough-ins, repair and replace fixtures, and disassemble or remove damaged or worn piping as necessary.
Load, unload, or identify building materials, machinery, or tools, and store materials appropriately.
Append protective coverings or fill cracks areas with putty or epoxy compounds in surfaces requiring repair.
Clear drains and downspouts and clean gutters to maintain proper drainage and prevent obstructions.
Transport tools, equipment, and materials to work sites using hand or motorized equipment, ensuring safe distribution.
Signal equipment operators to facilitate alignment, movement, or adjustment of machinery, equipment, or materials as necessary.
Drive heavy equipment and vehicles, such as backhoes, to perform construction tasks efficiently and safely.
Inspect, clean, and repair drainage systems, bridges, tunnels, and other structures.
Haul and spread sand, gravel, and clay to fill washouts and repair road shoulders.
Erect, install, or repair guardrails, road shoulders, berms, highway markers, warning signals, and highway lighting, using hand tools and power tools.
Remove litter and debris from roadways, including debris from rock and mud slides.
Clean and clear debris from culverts, catch basins, drop inlets, ditches, and other drain structures.
Perform roadside landscaping work, such as clearing weeds and brush, and planting and trimming trees.
Lubricate machines, change oil, or fill hydraulic reservoirs to specified levels and clean or make minor repairs to machines or equipment.
Clean tracks or clear ice or snow from tracks or switch boxes, and clean, grade, or level ballast on railroad tracks.
Drive graders, tamping machines, brooms, or ballast spreading machines to redistribute gravel or ballast between rails.
Observe leveling indicator arms to verify levelness and alignment of tracks, and adjust controls of machines that spread, shape, raise, level, or align track, according to specifications.
